Foundations · Chapter 1

A documentation starter that feels like a good book

Loomis Docs is a calm, editorial template for guides, references, and changelogs. Write like you'd write to a friend — your readers will thank you.

Welcome

Most documentation is written for the writer. Loomis flips that: every page is built to be read at a comfortable pace, with generous line height, a serif voice, and just enough structure to keep readers oriented.

This starter ships with a sidebar, table of contents, search, callouts, code blocks, and pagination — everything you need to publish your first guide today.

i

Made for writers

The components are intentionally simple. If you can write a paragraph, you can write a doc page.

Philosophy

Three rules guide every component in this template. First, words come first — visuals serve the prose, not the other way around. Second, surfaces should feel warm and familiar, like good paper. Third, navigation should disappear when you're reading and reappear the moment you need it.

Quick start

Browse the sidebar to begin. Installation walks you through getting a local copy running; Writing Content covers voice and structure; Theming shows how to make the palette your own.

Next steps

Each page in the sidebar is a real example you can copy and adapt. Press ⌘K at any time to jump between them.

Next up Installation
